Phase-53.NAME: User Cabinet Matrix Redesign
Phase-53.summary: Redesign the full user frontend into the approved KrotPN Matrix style while preserving compact mobile workflows and existing backend contracts.

constraints.constraint-1: No backend API shape changes, auth behavior changes, VPN provisioning changes, MTProto provisioning changes, billing logic changes, or deploy/install changes.
constraints.constraint-2: Primary user workflows must remain reachable on phone viewports without horizontal scroll or large decorative dashboard sections.
constraints.constraint-3: Matrix effects must not hide proxy secrets, VPN config actions, tariff prices, subscription countdown, or password-recovery states.

step-1.action: Audit current user route hierarchy and map every page to Matrix compact surfaces.
step-1.acceptance: Route-by-route UI target matrix exists before edits.

step-2.action: Redesign login, register, check-email, verify-email, forgot-password, and reset-password screens with readable Matrix forms.
step-2.acceptance: Auth flows keep existing API calls, password guidance, duplicate-email recovery, spam hint, and empty admin-free defaults.

step-3.action: Redesign dashboard around VPN status, pending/active trial countdown, MTProto proxy, and primary config actions.
step-3.acceptance: VPN and MTProto actions are visible within one or two taps on mobile.

step-4.action: Redesign VPN config route with compact QR, copy, download, device create, rotate, and revoke surfaces.
step-4.acceptance: .conf download behavior and device API behavior remain unchanged.

step-5.action: Redesign subscription route with Matrix tariff cards, countdown, active date calendar, and payment CTA hierarchy.
step-5.acceptance: Canonical 1/6/9 tariff prices and device limits remain server-authoritative and readable.

step-6.action: Redesign referrals and settings routes with compact rows, clear status states, and safe long-text wrapping.
step-6.acceptance: Long emails, referral links, and device names fit without overlap.

step-7.action: Normalize loading, empty, success, warning, and error states to Matrix visual primitives.
step-7.acceptance: State changes do not resize fixed controls or hide primary actions.

step-8.action: Create Phase-53 user smoke covering mobile routes, desktop routes, reduced-motion, canvas visibility, no overlap, and no horizontal scroll.

step-9.action: Run frontend build and screenshot verification across mobile and desktop viewports.
step-9.acceptance: Frontend build and viewport smoke pass.

step-10.action: Update MyGRACE docs, module maps, verification evidence, and current status after implementation.
step-10.acceptance: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff check, and protected deploy/install guard pass.

mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-1: New user registers: Matrix styling is visible, password guidance is readable, email confirmation guidance remains clear, and no stale VPN promise appears before verification.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-2: Verified user opens dashboard on mobile: VPN status, config action, MTProto proxy, and subscription countdown are visible without scrolling through decorative sections.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-3: User downloads config on Android/iOS browser: redesigned controls still call the hardened download path and keep .conf filename behavior intact.
